suggestions.title: Enable Medium publishing
suggestions.description: Activate the Medium article channel to double content reach and earnings
how_to: 1. Generate a Medium integration token at https://medium.com/me/settings
2. Add the token as a GitHub secret named MEDIUM_INTEGRATION_TOKEN
3. Ensure the bot has the "articles_medium" feature enabled in the status

suggestions.title: Activate Twitter thread posting
suggestions.description: Post X/Twitter threads for audience growth and affiliate earnings
how_to: 1. Create a Twitter developer app and obtain API key, secret, access token, and access secret
2. Add each as GitHub secrets: TWITTER_API_KEY, TWITTER_API_SECRET, TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN, TWITTER_ACCESS_SECRET
3. Enable the "twitter" feature in the bot status

suggestions.title: Add unit tests for core modules
suggestions.description: Increase reliability by automatically testing article generation fallback and earnings aggregation
how_to: 1. Ensure Python's unittest module is available (built‑in)
2. The tests run automatically each cycle via the existing CI configuration
3. Review test results in the GitHub Actions logs

upgrade.purpose: codebase evolution, patch planning, and repair

research.purpose: article research briefs and second-opinion ideation

post.purpose: new article and social post generation

opportunities.title: Starter template compatibility repair
opportunities.reason: clean-checkout install/build proof fits automated AI patching; template compatibility fixes are easy for maintainers to review
opportunities.next_step: Pick one starter repo with a failing quickstart, capture the install/build error, patch the dependency or command, and offer the cleanup at a fixed price.

opportunities.title: Package migration cleanup for small Python projects
opportunities.reason: public proof makes this suitable for automated AI patching; CI/test work is concrete and easy for maintainers to accept
opportunities.next_step: Find one outdated dependency path, reproduce the breakage, and propose a fixed-price cleanup.

opportunities.title: Deprecated GitHub Actions cleanup
opportunities.reason: public proof makes this suitable for automated AI patching; CI/test work is concrete and easy for maintainers to accept
opportunities.next_step: Find one outdated dependency path, reproduce the breakage, and propose a fixed-price cleanup.

opportunities.title: Flaky test triage for tiny open-source maintainers
opportunities.reason: public proof makes this suitable for automated AI patching; CI/test work is concrete and easy for maintainers to accept
opportunities.next_step: Open the latest failed job, capture the failure signature, and patch only the failing path.

opportunities.title: Broken README examples in niche SDK repos
opportunities.reason: public proof makes this suitable for automated AI patching; working docs convert into trust quickly
opportunities.next_step: Run the documented example from a clean checkout and submit the corrected command or snippet.

requirements: Prefer work that can be reproduced from public logs, docs, or a clean checkout in under 30 minutes.
Prefer boring maintenance where the failure and expected fix are visible without private context.
Require a deterministic command, log, docs page, or issue thread that an AI agent can use as proof.
Keep the first contribution small enough for the bot to patch, test, and explain automatically.
Do not count discovery or speculative upside as earnings.
focus: failing CI with a small, reproducible fix
documentation examples that no longer run
test flakiness with a clear failure signature
type hints, packaging metadata, and release automation
small compatibility fixes in niche developer tools
abandoned but still-installed packages with open compatibility issues
template repos and starter kits whose quickstarts fail on current runtimes
internal-tool shaped repos where businesses need maintenance more than novelty
release-note gaps after breaking API changes
low-glamour data import/export bugs in small SaaS integrations
strategy_playbook: Start from maintenance pain, not idea novelty.
Use proof as the sales asset: failing command, failing log line, short before/after note.
Favor repeatable chores that can become productized services.
Look for AI-automatable chores: stale issues with logs, forks with small fixes, unanswered install failures.
Bundle adjacent fixes only after trust exists.
Treat content as deal flow from solved niche issues.
avoid_patterns: Large rewrites, vague feature requests, design taste debates, and architecture arguments without a failing proof.
Repos with no maintainer response, no recent users, no releases, and no business signal.
Crowded prize or beginner issues where many contributors compete for low-value visibility.
Unpaid speculative requests that need private context before value can be proven.
Crypto/NFT hype work unless there is a concrete paid maintenance task and bounded risk.
